title: Bridge Essay: Psilocybin triggers an activity-dependent rewiring of large-scale cortical networks.
concept: Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.

**Psilocybin triggers an activity-dependent rewiring of large-scale cortical networks.** - Cell (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.cell.2025.11.009. PMID: 41352354. Psilocybin holds promise as a treatment for mental illnesses. One dose of psilocybin induces structural remodeling of dendritic spines in the medial frontal cortex in mice. The dendritic spines would be innervated by presynaptic neurons, but the sources of these inputs have not been identified. Here, using monosynaptic rabies tracing, we map the brain-wide distribution of inputs to frontal cortical pyramidal neurons.
